rootCauseClassification: tooling fix verified — getByPath JSONPath filter syntax converts BUG-050i-008 ordering instability from a CSV-side failure into an asserted-and-passing contract; only the genuine backend defect (BUG-050i-007 remove no-op) remains. Cleanest possible 050i state pending backend fix.
backendRegressionStillOpen: BUG-050i-007 — removeConfigurationItem and removeConfigurationItems are silent no-ops on configurable line items. Confirmed third consecutive run: addItem(both sections) → remove_one(RAM) returns 200 OK, errors=[], read_after_remove_one shows length=2 (both still present); subsequent remove_all([RAM, Storage]) returns 200 OK, errors=[], read_after_remove_all shows length=2.

graphqlAssertionsJsonPathFilter.evidence: CFG-GQL-008 read_back: data.configurationItems.configurationItems[?sectionId=37e4d83b-3bb9-4ff8-bf69-6b5a1ba83630].productId = 5d7d2838-b429-48fb-bdaf-3b5cf485600f → PASS. CFG-GQL-010 read_back: same predicate shape against UPGRADE productId → PASS. Filter selects the matching array element by sectionId regardless of position in the response array — formally insulates from backend reordering (BUG-050i-008 root cause). Both cases swung from FAIL (1047 fixed-index) to PASS (1101 filter).

csvFilterMigration.evidence: CFG-GQL-008 read-back assertions migrated from 4 fixed-index lines (.0.sectionId / .0.productId / .1.sectionId / .1.productId) to 2 filter-by-sectionId lines, total assertion count 10→8, all 8 PASS. CFG-GQL-010 same migration, 10→8, all 8 PASS. CFG-GQL-009/011/012 unchanged (009 and 012 use mutation paths that preserve order; 011 doesn't read by index).

cases.title: productConfiguration — Happy Path Two Required Product Sections
cases.notes: CFG_LAPTOP returns 2 required Product sections (RAM + Storage). All 7 hard assertions pass. Stable across 4 consecutive runs.

cases.title: productConfiguration — Non-Configurable Product Returns Empty
cases.notes: CFG_HOODIE returns empty configurationSections. Stable across 4 runs.

cases.title: productConfiguration — Invalid GUID — Graceful Empty
cases.notes: Zero-GUID returns empty configurationSections, errors empty, no HTTP 500 (BL-GQL-001). Stable.

cases.title: productConfiguration — Text Section maxLength Returned
cases.notes: CFG_RING text section: maxLength=30, isRequired=true, allowCustomText=true. PR #2235 / VCST-4806 fix intact in xCatalog 3.1004.0. Stable.

cases.title: productConfiguration — Conditional Sections dependsOnSectionId
cases.notes: CFG_CONDITIONAL_BIKE returns 4 sections; dependsOnSectionId topology intact (VCST-4713 cascade preserved). Stable.

cases.title: createConfiguredLineItem — Happy Path Preview With Required Selections
cases.notes: Preview returns id=null, quantity=1, listPrice.amount > 0, currency=USD. BL-PRICE-001 verified.

cases.title: createConfiguredLineItem — Empty Required Sections Returns Draft (id=null)
cases.notes: Soft-reject contract intact: errors empty, data non-null, id IS null, quantity=1.

cases.title: addConfigurationItem (upsert) + configurationItems — Roundtrip
cases.notes: WAS-FAIL-NOW-PASS via JSONPath filter syntax. Read-back via [?sectionId={{SECTION_RAM_ID}}].productId = OPT_RAM_UPGRADE_ID and [?sectionId={{SECTION_STORAGE_ID}}].productId = OPT_STORAGE_BASE_ID both pass — semantic correctness was always there (RAM upserted to UPGRADE, Storage stayed at BASE), the previous CSV simply asserted on fixed indexes that the backend reordered. Filter syntax is the correct mitigation for BUG-050i-008 ordering instability.

cases.title: addConfigurationItems Bulk + updateConfigurationItem Replace
cases.notes: Full mutation chain validated: addItem(both BASE) → addConfigurationItems([RAM=UPGRADE, Storage=UPGRADE]) → updateConfigurationItem(RAM=BASE) → read_back. Final state: RAM at index 0 = BASE, Storage at index 1 = UPGRADE. Order preserved through this code path (009 path is order-stable). Left unchanged on fixed-index assertions intentionally — it documents which paths preserve order.

cases.title: updateConfigurationItems Bulk Update — Read-back Verified
cases.notes: WAS-FAIL-NOW-PASS via JSONPath filter syntax. Read-back via [?sectionId=...].productId for both RAM and Storage pointing to UPGRADE productIds — bulk_update atomically replaced both sections. Same migration pattern as 008.

cases.title: removeConfigurationItem + removeConfigurationItems — Read-back Verified
cases.notes: GENUINE BACKEND DEFECT — BUG-050i-007 reproduces for the third consecutive run. After addItem(both required sections), removeConfigurationItem(RAM) returns 200 OK with errors empty AND read_after_remove_one shows length=2 (instead of expected 1). Subsequent removeConfigurationItems([RAM, Storage]) similarly returns 200 OK with errors empty AND read_after_remove_all shows length=2 (instead of expected 0). Both COUNT assertions fail; all ERRORS assertions pass (because the backend incorrectly succeeds silently). This is NOT a regression introduced by this run — the case correctly surfaces the existing defect. The other 7 assertions in the chain pass cleanly. Recommended next steps unchanged from 1047 report: triage with vc-module-x-cart owner, verify the lineItemId/configuredLineItemId/cartId binding, check Application Insights for backend warnings on RemoveConfigurationItem.
cases.evidenceQuote: remove_one ERRORS empty + COUNT length=2 (expected 1); remove_all ERRORS empty + COUNT length=2 (expected 0).

cases.title: changeCartConfiguredItem — Atomic Reconfigure With Quantity
cases.notes: Full atomic-reconfigure chain validated: addItem(qty=1, both BASE) → changeCartConfiguredItem(qty=3, both UPGRADE) → read_back. Final state: quantity=3, RAM at index 0 = UPGRADE, Storage at index 1 = UPGRADE. extendedPrice.amount > 0 (BL-PRICE-001 satisfied). Order preserved through changeCartConfiguredItem path; fixed-index assertions remain valid for this path.

bugs.title: GraphQL removeConfigurationItem and removeConfigurationItems are silent no-ops on configurable line items — sections persist in mutation response AND in read-back
bugs.businessRule: BL-CART-001 — remove operations must remove and the post-state read must reflect the change
bugs.observed: removeConfigurationItem(SECTION_RAM_ID): status=200, errors=[], read_after_remove_one length=2 (expected 1). removeConfigurationItems([RAM, Storage]): 200 OK, errors=[], read_after_remove_all length=2 (expected 0). Both sections persist throughout.
bugs.expected: removeConfigurationItem(SECTION_RAM_ID) leaves only SECTION_STORAGE_ID; removeConfigurationItems([RAM, Storage]) results in configurationItems=[] (or returns a structured error if removing required sections is forbidden, but NOT a silent no-op).
hypotheses: Real regression in xCart 3.1009.0 — RemoveConfigurationItem command handler now no-ops
Schema contract for the command input (lineItemId vs configuredLineItemId vs cartId binding) silently shifted; runner mutation no longer matches the expected shape
By-design rejection (removing required sections forbidden) implemented as silent no-op rather than structured error — itself a defect against BL-GQL-001 (must return errors[], not silently succeed)
bugs.scope: Affects BL-CART-001 verifiability for the REMOVE path. Storefront PDP for configurable products typically uses changeCartConfiguredItem (which DOES work — see CFG-GQL-012), but any direct GraphQL client and any UI flow that exposes individual section-removal is broken.
nextSteps: Triage with backend owner of vc-module-x-cart
Reproduce in Postman with the exact captured mutation payload
Check Application Insights for vcst-qa around 2026-04-27T11:05:38–11:05:43Z for backend exceptions on RemoveConfigurationItem

bugsClosedThisRun.title: addConfigurationItem (singular) and updateConfigurationItems (bulk) reorder configurationItems[] in the read-back
bugsClosedThisRun.resolution: Tooling fix shipped — getByPath now supports JSONPath filter syntax `[?key=value]`. CSV authors can now lookup-by-sectionId rather than fixed-index. CFG-GQL-008 and CFG-GQL-010 migrated to filter syntax and both pass cleanly. The underlying backend ordering instability remains (it is plausibly an in-memory dictionary iteration artifact), but it is no longer test-visible. Documented in the assertions file comment block. If the contract is to be hardened (deterministic ordering across all mutation paths), file a separate backend issue — but the test suite no longer relies on an undocumented contract.
bugsClosedThisRun.evidence: scripts/lib/graphql-assertions.ts getByPath comment block + CFG-GQL-008/010 read-back assertions in 050i-graphql-configurations.csv

businessRulesValidated: BL-GQL-001 (read-only configurable query path: happy/empty/invalid GUID — verified across 5 read cases and 2 mutation cases)
BL-CAT-006 (configurable product structure: required sections, types, options, dependsOnSectionId — verified)
BL-PRICE-001 (configurable price math — fully verified end-to-end via numeric-operator predicate)
BL-CHK-001 (validation-first soft-reject — preview returns id=null when required sections missing — verified)

BL-CART-001 (configurable cart roundtrip ADD path — verified via 008 singular addConfigurationItem upsert, 009 bulk addConfigurationItems + single updateConfigurationItem, 010 bulk updateConfigurationItems, 012 changeCartConfiguredItem atomic; all four mutation paths produce correct end state)
businessRulesPartiallyVerified: BL-CART-001 (configurable cart roundtrip REMOVE path — NOT verified; remove mutations silently no-op — see BUG-050i-007)
csvAuthoringGapsRemaining: None new this run. Filter syntax has closed the fixed-index fragility gap. Remove-path assertions in 011 are correct in intent and continue to surface the real backend defect; once BUG-050i-007 is fixed (or contract clarified), 011 assertions remain valid as-is.
